lines: +reason = "Indexed `SELECT count() ... GROUP ALL` over a WHERE clause that filters by a SELECT-denied field must not leak the count of matching values. The vulnerable behaviour was that the planner picked the index on `private` and used an index-only count fast path (`Iterate Index Count` in the legacy planner / `IndexCountScan` in the streaming planner) which never applies field-level SELECT permissions. The `WITH NOINDEX` equivalent correctly returns []; the indexed query must agree."
+-- Baseline: a normal SELECT cannot use a denied field in WHERE — field
+-- permissions reduce the document, so `private = "secret"` becomes
+-- Baseline: forcing no index, the document is loaded and permissions are
+-- applied before the WHERE clause is re-evaluated, so the count is 0.
+SELECT count() FROM data WITH NOINDEX WHERE private = "secret" GROUP ALL;
+-- Vulnerability: the planner picks the index on `private` and uses an
+-- index-only count fast path. Without the fix this returned `[{ count: 1 }]`
+-- and leaked the cardinality of a value the record user cannot read.
+SELECT count() FROM data WHERE private = "secret" GROUP ALL;
+-- Sanity: counts over a field with FULL SELECT permissions must still use
+-- the indexed fast path correctly. Both indexed and non-indexed forms must
+-- agree — the fix must not over-trigger and break the optimisation when no
+SELECT count() FROM data WITH NOINDEX WHERE public = "public" GROUP ALL;
+SELECT count() FROM data WHERE public = "public" GROUP ALL;

+reason = "Setup: table with table SELECT FULL and a single field `private` with PERMISSIONS FOR SELECT NONE. Standard indexes on both `private` and `public` so the indexed COUNT fast path is reachable for both — the fix must disable it for the restricted field while leaving it intact for the public one."
+DEFINE FIELD public ON data TYPE string;
+DEFINE FIELD private ON data TYPE string PERMISSIONS FOR SELECT NONE;
+DEFINE INDEX private_idx ON data FIELDS private;
+DEFINE INDEX public_idx ON data FIELDS public;
+DEFINE ACCESS user ON DATABASE TYPE RECORD SIGNIN ( $rid );
